title: salix × pendulina
intro: Salix × pendulina, also known as weeping willow, is a hybrid plant derived from the cross between Salix alba and Salix fragilis. The bark and leaves of Salix × pendulina contain salicylic acid, which has been used for its medicinal properties, including reducing inflammation and relieving pain. The plant's health benefits include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate headaches, and soothe digestive issues. Salix × pendulina can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of ailments, although it is essential to consult a medical professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
